perturbation.original_text: 1.5 The term "Products" shall mean those certain products, specified by brand name, manufactured and marketed under the Trademarks by STAAR that are listed on Exhibit A-1 hereto. STAAR may from time to time modify or change the Products on Exhibit A-1 in its sole discretion, and if so, will notify Distributor promptly thereof. In the event that STAAR discontinues a Product, it will provide at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice to Distributor. Distributor shall not modify, disassemble or reverse engineer any Product, in whole or in part.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 The term "Products" shall initially mean those certain products, specified by brand name, manufactured and marketed under the Trademarks by STAAR that are listed on Exhibit A-1 hereto. However, STAAR may unilaterally and at any time add or remove products from the definition of "Products" without any notice to or consent from Distributor. Distributor shall not modify, disassemble or reverse engineer any Product, in whole or in part.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows STAAR to modify the definition of 'Products' at any time without notice, contradicting the initial understanding of 'Products' being limited to Exhibit A-1. This creates uncertainty because the rest of the contract relies on a fixed definition of 'Products'. For example, Section 7.3 requires the purchase of Minimum Product Quantities, which will be confusing, since if STAAR unilaterally updates the defintion of Products, there is no clarification on the distributors responsiblity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.3 During each Contract Year, as defined below, Distributor shall purchase from STAAR the minimum quantity of each Product that shall be mutually agreed between the parties in advance of the applicable Contract Year ("Minimum Product Quantities"). The Minimum Purchase Quantities for the Contract Year are as set forth on Exhibit B attached hereto. Within ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of each Contract Year, the parties will discuss in good faith and agree on the Minimum Product Quantities for the successive Contract Year; provided, however, that, if the parties fail to reach agreement on or otherwise specify the Minimum Purchase Quantities for the successive Contract Year, the Minimum Product Quantities for such successive Contract Year shall be __________ percent (___%) of the Minimum Purchase Quantities for the existing Contract Year. "Contract Year" means for the first Contract Year of the Agreement, the period commencing on the Effective Date hereof and ending one year thereafter and for subsequent Contract Years, the successive twelve (12) month period thereafter. Failure of Distributor to purchase the Minimum Purchase Quantities for any Contract Year, shall be considered a material breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows STAAR to unilaterally modify the definition of 'Products' without notice, which directly contradicts section 7.3. Section 7.3 requires the Distributor to purchase 'Minimum Product Quantities' of each 'Product', as mutually agreed upon. However, the changed text allows STAAR to change what constitutes a 'Product' at any time, without agreement from Distributor. This creates an uncertainty and contradiction regarding the purchase obligations of the Distributor as outlined in Section 7.3, making it unclear what the 'Minimum Product Quantities' actually refer to.

perturbation.original_text: 8.3 All Products ordered pursuant to accepted purchase orders shall be scheduled for delivery in accordance with STAAR's then current and normal delivery times.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.3 All Products ordered pursuant to accepted purchase orders shall be scheduled for delivery at STAAR's sole discretion, irrespective of STAAR's then current and normal delivery times.
perturbation.explanation: This change gives STAAR complete control over delivery schedules, potentially contradicting other clauses that assume reasonable or predictable delivery times. This can create issues in the context of Section 6.2, regarding the distributor's obligations for stocking the product, as it will be hard to stock with unpridectible delivery times.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Employ adequate staff having specialized technical training, maintain adequate stocks of Products, and maintain facilities and other resources within the Territory, at its own expense, in order to maximize the distribution and sale of the Products;
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8.3 grants STAAR sole discretion over delivery schedules, regardless of their standard delivery times. This directly contradicts Section 6.2, which requires the distributor to 'maintain adequate stocks of Products' to maximize sales. If delivery times are unpredictable and solely at STAAR's discretion, the distributor cannot effectively maintain adequate stock levels, creating a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 13.2 Distributor shall, at its own expense, maintain at a minimum general and product liability coverage in the Territory of at least US$2 million per occurrence, US$5 million in the aggregate. On a separate endorsement, Distributor shall name STAAR as an additional named insured. Such separate endorsement shall indicate that Distributor's insurance is primary and that STAAR's coverage as an additional named insured is not contributory. Each such insurance policy and endorsement shall provide that the insurance will not be canceled or reduces without at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice to STAAR. On request, Distributor shall provide STAAR with copies or certificates of all such insurance policies.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.2 Distributor shall, at its own expense, maintain general and product liability coverage in the Territory as deemed sufficient by the Distributor, with no minimum coverage amount required. On a separate endorsement, Distributor shall name STAAR as an additional named insured. Such separate endorsement shall indicate that Distributor's insurance is primary and that STAAR's coverage as an additional named insured is not contributory. Each such insurance policy and endorsement shall provide that the insurance will not be canceled or reduces without at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice to STAAR. On request, Distributor shall provide STAAR with copies or certificates of all such insurance policies.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the explicit minimum insurance coverage requirements, now deeming them as sufficient by the Distributor. The creates ambiguity when referencing 13.1 which talks about Indemnification, since there are no guidelines for the insurance to follow.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.1 Distributor shall indemnify, defend with competent and experienced counsel and hold harmless STAAR, and its officers, directors, employees, affiliates and agents (each, a "Indemnities"), from and against any and all claims, demands, suits or actions (including without limitation attorneys' fees and disbursements) which may be asserted against STAAR for any kind of damages, including without limitation damage or injury to property or persons and incidental and consequential damages, by any third party or any of the Indemnities arising out of, in connection with or resulting from (a) any breach of any representation or performance obligation of Distributor hereunder, or (b) any act or omission of Distributor, its employees, agents or representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 13.2 removes the minimum insurance coverage requirements, stating that the coverage is 'as deemed sufficient by the Distributor, with no minimum coverage amount required.' This directly contradicts 13.1, which requires the Distributor to indemnify STAAR against claims and damages. Without a minimum coverage amount, the ability of the Distributor's insurance to adequately cover potential liabilities under the indemnification clause becomes uncertain and potentially insufficient, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The change introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty, adhering to the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
